digest.component_or_area: nava-rebar release workflow and version manifests
propositions: Cut the v0.8.0 minor release of nava-rebar, bumping from 0.7.1 to 0.8.0 across all version manifests and the enforced doc marker in lockstep.
Promote the CHANGELOG [Unreleased] section to [0.8.0] with a fresh empty [Unreleased] and updated footer compare links.
docs/releasing.md still documents the old 'git push origin main' flow, contradicting the Gerrit-only landing rule, and must be corrected to the Gerrit two-vote flow.
The release.yml authorize job asserts version-lockstep and only publishes from a workflow_dispatch-on-main run whose version input matches the manifests.
Land the bump commit on main through the Gerrit two-vote gate (LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1), then dispatch release.yml with version=0.8.0 to completion.
PyPI publishes are immutable, so a bad release must be yanked and re-cut as 0.8.1; only failed post-publish jobs should be re-run, never the whole workflow.
